name: San Francisco Fire
description_short: San Francisco Fire Credit Union is a state-chartered credit union established in 1959, serving over 79,300 members with $1.7 billion in assets across one branch and 3 ATMs in San Francisco.
description_long:
San Francisco Fire Credit Union was established in 1959 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California. As a state-chartered credit union, it operates under California regulations and serves the San Francisco Bay Area community. The institution has grown to steward over $1.7 billion in assets while maintaining a membership base of more than 79,300 loyal members supported by over 200 dedicated employees.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs, credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, business loans, and mortgage options (fixed-rate, adjustable-rate, and balloon/hybrid mortgages). Members can access digital banking through online banking and mobile apps available on iOS and Android platforms, with services including bill pay, e-statements, external account transfers, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and online loan payments.

San Francisco Fire distinguishes itself as a mission-focused credit union with a strong community presence. The institution provides financial counseling, financial education programs, and financial workshops to members. It also offers student scholarships, indicating a commitment to community development beyond traditional banking services. The credit union maintains physical presence with one main office located at 3201 California Street and provides access through 3 ATMs across the service area.

The primary limitation is the minimal branch footprint—only one physical branch and three ATMs—which may inconvenience members seeking in-person service or cash access outside the immediate San Francisco area. Additionally, the website shows intermittent service status notifications and outage reporting capabilities, suggesting potential reliability concerns with online and mobile platforms that members should verify before enrollment. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with bill pay, e-statements, and external transfers
pros: Over $1.7 billion in assets provides institutional stability and resource depth
Serves 79,300+ members with 65+ years of operational history since 1959
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, business loans, and credit cards
Digital banking with mobile apps for iOS and Android with remote deposit capture
Community-focused programs including financial counseling, workshops, scholarships, and financial education
Member account diversity shows strong engagement (63,400+ share drafts, 46,300+ money market accounts)
Online calculators for mortgages and auto loans to help with financial planning
cons: Only 1 physical branch and 3 ATMs limits accessibility for non-local or frequent in-person banking needs
Website displays service status warnings and outage reporting pages, indicating potential platform stability issues
No customer reviews currently available on the listed profile, making third-party satisfaction assessment difficult
Limited geographic footprint restricted to California with state charter, reducing options for members relocating outside state
best_for: San Francisco Bay Area residents seeking full-service credit union banking with community values
Small business owners needing business loans and comprehensive banking from a mission-driven institution
Long-term members building savings through multiple account types (savings, money market, CDs) with financial counseling support
Auto and mortgage borrowers in California seeking competitive rates from a stable, member-owned institution
diagnosis: San Francisco Fire Credit Union is best for established residents of the San Francisco Bay Area seeking full-service credit union banking with community involvement, financial education, and member-focused rates. The primary caveat is the severely limited branch footprint (1 branch, 3 ATMs), which makes this impractical for members requiring frequent in-person service or those outside the immediate San Francisco area, and website service alerts suggest potential digital banking reliability concerns that should be verified.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
